transcript.clean_text: the calm after the sprinting storm not a bit of it this was going to be a very dramatic stage number 12 of lovuelta after the sprint the previous day the team caden groves after what was one of the slowest days so far come through for the quick men at the very last no change overall the gap still the same between ever nepal roglic and mass going into a day with a big mountains test but only at the very end of it rolling terrain up until that point high temperatures once again and a bit of a carnival atmosphere not least on the quick step alpha vinyl bus with news that julian ama philippe was not too seriously hurt nothing broken a dislocated shoulder and ligament damage he should be back evan appalled rolled out smiling in the red jersey he was to be upset through the day but how would he go down the coast they went with the big climb at the very end defining the day so for the fact that it was made for a breakaway who would fancy it plenty is the answer in the end only four teams did not make the break it was huge looking like the peloton itself 32 riders managing to pull away with a chance of glory well it was too big to go right to the line bantistella finished run up on two stages decided he was going to roll the dice with 65 kilometers to go nobody was tempted to join him doing a lot of work out there where ups and de koenig tamino and vimesh working for jay vine and the terrain here working its magic again sometimes black magic i think you might say having a pole on the deck this time remounting not happy issuing his complaints and meanwhile ballistella issuing a couple of oaths as well why didn't anyone come and join me well sometimes you just have to do it for yourself and you only have to ask evan nepal about that how would he respond to the inevitable attacks that would come on the final climb and so it began immediately after the intermediate sprint in the break fabro was working for kelderman doing a great job for boro hansgrower but as the pace picked up it was having a big cost jan palance one of the best place was starting to struggle meanwhile yomovisma broglich team started to rain down blows upon evertopol the leader movie star joined the fun ever nepal with teammates gradually running out had his own battle setting fair out front elijah's bear from the break and jay vine maybe the fatigue of winning two stages of this race so far finally taking a toll so it got selective out front polance was holding on to enhance his position overall gelderman was riding well karapas still involved while the battle amongst the gc men raged on constantly questions were asked one man had the answer for the stage win and that was richard cara pass coming here as team principal for inies grenadiers questions had been asked of him indeed the pecking order changed within his team a stage win well that would quiet down some of those words frustration and relief in equal measure as carapace took the stage but what about those in his wake and indeed the gc battle was raging on up the mountain caldermann moving into the top six with this performance on the day good finish for solo as well meanwhile evan apoll well this was all about making a statement at the very last but he picked himself up out of the saddle and started to charge for home ellen mcmaster primos roglic close at hand not that far away either carlos rodriguez spanish champion but ever nepal it was that crossed the line first as if to say i may have been bruised but i am not bowed neither is richard cara pass getting his mojo back and all stage wins a potential for him deeper into this race caravas took it ahead of kellum and moves into the top six as we said solaire third ahead of polansku recovered brilliantly brenner jesbeh jay vine finished seventh ahead of hagen shaw and fabro who've done a lot of pacing for kelderman on this climb quick finish most certainly one of the fastest schedules that we have run after the slowest the day prior what is left for this man ever nepal leads by the same margin as his day began despite the fall too much from 41 ahead of roglich three mentioned three ahead of mass but kelvin up to six at berlant up to ninth overall well with high ground to come in the mountains anything can change what about stage 13 unlucky for some well quite possibly they run down all the way to the line an intermediate sprint and a kick up at the end you have to ride your luck in this race let's hope everyone stays lucky as we take on stage 13. you
